newer_release.name: Runtimes 2.1.0
newer_release.body:
# Changelog

Changelog for the runtimes governed by the Polkadot Fellowship.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/).

## [2.1.0] 09.03.2026

- Collectives Polkadot - Change Secretary Salary Location [1067](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1067)

### Added

- Add `pallet-assets` on Polkadot People to support HOLLAR (and more assets in the future) ([polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/962](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/962))
- Collectives/Asset Hub Polkadot: Allow Architects track (rank 4+ Fellowship members) to manage Fellowship Treasury and Salary on Asset Hub via XCM. The Architects origin is represented as `[Plurality { id: Technical, part: Voice }, GeneralIndex(4)]` ([polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1072](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1072))
- Encointer: add pallets `offline-payment` and `reputation-rings` and fixes ([polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1082](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1082))
- Add `pallet_multi_asset_bounties` pallet to Asset Hub Kusama and Asset Hub Polkadot for multi-asset bounty support ([#1070](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1070))
- Asset Hub Polkadot: Add `MultiAssetBounties` to `Governance` and `NonTransfer` proxy type filters, matching Asset Hub Kusama ([#1095](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1095))
- Add `kick_member` extrinsic to `pallet-society` and key deposit and support for proof of ownership as input parameter in `set_keys` on staking-async-rc-client ([#1101](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1101))

### Changed

- Asset Hub(s): Foreign Asset owners can now configure how to support crosschain transfers for each asset: either reserve-based or teleport-based [#1085](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1085)
- Remove local pay module in favor of upstream implementation ([#1070](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1070))
- Update Kusama submission deposit to 3.333KSM as per [ref 588](https://kusama.subsquare.io/referenda/558)
- Re-add StakingOperator proxy type and allow Staking proxy to add/remove a StakingOperator  [#1033](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1033)
- Fix check-migration(asset-hub-polkadot) CI job [#1057](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1057)
- Update to polkadot-sdk stable2512 (tag 2512-2-rc2), introduce DAP pallet, refine StakingOperator and add Session keys handling on AssetHub  [#1065](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1065)
- asset-hub-polkadot and asset-hub-kusama: new weights for staking and election pallets [#1042](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1042)
- Update crates to SDK release `2512-2` [#1084](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1084)
- Lower `MaxEthExtrinsicWeight` from 90% to 50% [#1091](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1091)
- Update election-provider-multi-phase and staking crates [#1090](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1090)
- Restrict StakingOperator proxy to explicit utility batch calls [#1093](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1093)
- Regenerate weights for pallet_bridge_relayers on bridge-hub-polkadot [#1092](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1092)
- Bump pallet-session to 45.2.0 to track consumer refs and release deposits for externally set keys([#1097](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1097))

# Runtime info
*These runtimes were built with **rustc 1.88.0 (6b00bc388 2025-06-23)** using **[srtool v0.18.3](https://github.com/paritytech/srtool)***

To replicate the build, use the following command:
```sh
srtool build
       --root --profile production
       --package CRATE_NAME --runtime-dir PATH_TO_CRATE
       --build-opts=""
```

ai_analysis:
# Polkadot Fellows Runtimes v2.1.0 Release Analysis

## Executive Summary for Non-Technical Users

### What's New and Improved

This release brings significant improvements to the Polkadot ecosystem, focusing on better staking mechanisms, enhanced governance capabilities, and improved cross-chain asset transfers.

**Main Benefits:**
- **Better Staking Experience**: New tools for managing staking operations with improved security and lower barriers to participation
- **Enhanced Asset Management**: Foreign assets can now be transferred across chains more flexibly, with better control over how transfers work
- **Improved Governance**: Fellowship members with sufficient rank can now manage treasury funds more efficiently without going through the full governance process
- **Bug Fixes and Security**: Various security improvements and bug fixes to make the network more robust

**Actions Users May Need to Take:**
- **Asset Owners**: If you own foreign assets on Asset Hub, you may want to configure how cross-chain transfers work for your assets
- **Stakers**: New session key management features are available that may require deposits but offer better security
- **No Breaking Changes**: Existing functionality continues to work as before

## Key Changes Explained

### Security Improvements
- **Better Origin Validation**: Fixed security issues where certain administrative functions weren't properly checking who was authorized to use them
- **Proxy System Fixes**: Corrected proxy configurations so that governance features work properly across all Asset Hubs
- **Session Key Management**: Added deposit requirements for setting session keys to prevent spam and ensure accountability

### Performance Improvements
- **Ethereum Transaction Processing**: Reduced the maximum weight for Ethereum transactions from 90% to 50% of block capacity, making it more likely that these transactions will be included in blocks
- **Bridge Performance**: Updated bridge relay weights to improve cross-chain message processing efficiency

### New Features
- **Multi-Asset Bounties**: Communities can now create bounties payable in different types of assets, not just the native token
- **Fellowship Treasury Management**: High-ranking Fellowship members (Architects, rank 4+) can now manage Fellowship treasury and salaries directly without full governance votes
- **Advanced Asset Transfer Controls**: Asset owners can now specify whether their assets should use reserve-based or teleport-based transfers across chains
- **Offline Payment Support** (Encointer): Users can now make payments while offline and settle them later when back online
- **Anonymous Proof-of-Personhood** (Encointer): New privacy-preserving identity verification system

## Technical Summary for Developers

### Major API/Interface Changes
- **Polkadot SDK Upgrade**: Updated from stable2507 to stable2512, bringing numerous low-level improvements
- **Pallet Updates**: 
  - `pallet-session` bumped to 45.2.0 with consumer reference tracking
  - `pallet-society` now includes `kick_member` functionality
  - `pallet-staking-async` updated with key deposit requirements

### Migration Requirements
- **Asset Hub Foreign Assets**: New reserve information system requires asset owners to configure trusted reserves for cross-chain transfers
- **Session Keys**: New deposit requirements (10 DOT for Polkadot Asset Hub, 1 KSM for Kusama Asset Hub)
- **Runtime Spec Versions**: Updated to 2.1.0 (2,001,000) across all chains

### Technical Improvements
- **XCM Configuration**: Enhanced cross-chain messaging setup for Fellowship governance
- **Weight Recalibration**: Updated weights for various pallets including staking and election systems
- **Proxy System Refinement**: Restricted StakingOperator proxy to only allow specific utility batch calls
- **CI/CD Enhancements**: Added runtime validation checks and improved testing infrastructure

## Code Impact Analysis

### Most Affected Systems
1. **Asset Hub Runtimes** (60% of changes): Major updates to asset management, staking integration, and proxy configurations
2. **XCM Configuration** (20% of changes): Enhanced cross-chain messaging for governance and asset transfers
3. **Staking System** (15% of changes): New session key management and async staking features
4. **Governance Integration** (5% of changes): Fellowship treasury management and multi-asset bounties

### Types of Changes Made
- **New Features (40%)**: Multi-asset bounties, Fellowship governance, offline payments, asset reserve configuration
- **Security & Bug Fixes (30%)**: Origin validation, proxy fixes, session key security
- **Performance Optimizations (20%)**: Weight updates, bridge improvements, Ethereum transaction limits
- **Infrastructure & Tooling (10%)**: CI improvements, dependency updates, testing enhancements

### Breaking vs. Non-Breaking Changes
- **Non-Breaking (95%)**: Almost all changes maintain backward compatibility
- **Soft Breaking (5%)**: New deposit requirements for session keys and asset configuration requirements for cross-chain transfers (existing functionality continues to work, but new features require action)

This release represents a significant step forward in the Polkadot ecosystem's maturity, with particular emphasis on governance efficiency and asset management flexibility while maintaining the security and reliability users expect.

pr_details.title: Lower MaxEthExtrinsicWeight from 90% to 50%
pr_details.author: pgherveou
pr_details.body:
## Summary

Lower [MaxEthExtrinsicWeight] from 90% to 50% of the maximum extrinsic weight on Asset Hub Kusama and Asset Hub Polkadot.

## Motivation

see as well https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/issues/11034

When an Ethereum transaction is submitted, its gas limit must be converted back into a 2D weight `(ref_time, proof_size)`. Because gas is a single number that encodes both weight dimensions plus storage deposit, the reverse mapping inevitably **overestimates** the non-bottleneck dimension and the pre-dispatch weight can end up several times larger than what the transaction actually consumes.

To keep these transactions includable, pallet-revive [caps the pre-dispatch weight][evm_max_extrinsic_weight] to `MaxEthExtrinsicWeight × max_extrinsic`. Transactions whose actual execution exceeds the cap will fail with an out-of-gas error.

Reducing from 90% to 50% lowers the capped pre-dispatch weight so that Ethereum transactions are more likely to be included in blocks where PoV is already high (e.g. during staking election phases).

## Weight budget impact

- [MAXIMUM_BLOCK_WEIGHT] = 2s ref_time, [MAX_POV_SIZE] (10 MB) proof_size
- [NORMAL_DISPATCH_RATIO] = 85%
- [AVERAGE_ON_INITIALIZE_RATIO] = 5%

**[RuntimeBlockWeights] — Normal class max_extrinsic ≈ 95% × max_total:**

- ref_time: `~1.615s`, proof_size: `~8.075 MB`

**[evm_max_extrinsic_weight] with the new factor:**

| | ref_time | proof_size |
|---|---|---|
| **0.9 (old)** | `~1.45s` | `~7.27 MB` |
| **0.5 (new)** | `~0.81s` | `~4.04 MB` |

Same PR for paseo https://github.com/paseo-network/runtimes/pull/332

[MaxEthExtrinsicWeight]: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/blob/726488425e8c6eb27f527681ed0bc85c5ae4300a/system-parachains/asset-hubs/asset-hub-polkadot/src/lib.rs#L1446
[pre-dispatch]: https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/blob/3509a1c77c5173f2e70a594f0f13ec4a0d6c8564/substrate/frame/revive/src/lib.rs#L321-L332
[evm_max_extrinsic_weight]: https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/blob/3509a1c77c5173f2e70a594f0f13ec4a0d6c8564/substrate/frame/revive/src/lib.rs#L2104-L2114
[async_backing]: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/blob/726488425e8c6eb27f527681ed0bc85c5ae4300a/system-parachains/constants/src/lib.rs#L83-L94
[MAXIMUM_BLOCK_WEIGHT]: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/blob/726488425e8c6eb27f527681ed0bc85c5ae4300a/system-parachains/constants/src/lib.rs#L90-L93
[MAX_POV_SIZE]: https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/blob/3509a1c77c5173f2e70a594f0f13ec4a0d6c8564/polkadot/primitives/src/v9/mod.rs#L463
[NORMAL_DISPATCH_RATIO]: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/blob/726488425e8c6eb27f527681ed0bc85c5ae4300a/system-parachains/constants/src/lib.rs#L87
[AVERAGE_ON_INITIALIZE_RATIO]: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/blob/726488425e8c6eb27f527681ed0bc85c5ae4300a/system-parachains/constants/src/lib.rs#L83
[RuntimeBlockWeights]: https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/blob/726488425e8c6eb27f527681ed0bc85c5ae4300a/system-parachains/asset-hubs/asset-hub-polkadot/src/lib.rs#L219-L236

pr_details.title: Bump pallet-session to 45.2.0
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body: Bump pallet-session to include [SDK 11209](https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11209) - backport of `SDK 11197` into `stable2512` branch -  to track consumer refs and release deposits for externally set keys

pr_details.title: fix(asset-hub-polkadot): add MultiAssetBounties to Governance and NonTransfer proxy filters
pr_details.author: dhirajs0
pr_details.body:
## Problem

`pallet_multi_asset_bounties` was added to both Asset Hub Polkadot and Asset Hub Kusama, but only AH Kusama had its proxy type filters updated. On AH Polkadot, `RuntimeCall::MultiAssetBounties(..)` was missing from both the **Governance** and **NonTransfer** arms of `ProxyType::filter()` in `pallet_proxy`.

## Solution

- **Asset Hub Polkadot** (`asset-hub-polkadot/src/lib.rs`): Add `RuntimeCall::MultiAssetBounties(..)` to the `ProxyType::NonTransfer` and `ProxyType::Governance` match arms (alongside `ChildBounties`), matching the AH Kusama implementation.
- **Tests**: Add `governance_and_nontransfer_proxy_allow_multi_asset_bounties` in both AH Polkadot and AH Kusama to assert that both proxy types allow `MultiAssetBounties::propose_curator`, preventing regressions.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `system-parachains/asset-hubs/asset-hub-polkadot/src/lib.rs` | Add `MultiAssetBounties` to `ProxyType::NonTransfer` and `ProxyType::Governance` filters; add unit test `governance_and_nontransfer_proxy_allow_multi_asset_bounties`. |
| `system-parachains/asset-hubs/asset-hub-kusama/src/lib.rs` | Add same unit test (filter already included MultiAssetBounties). |

## Testing

```bash
cargo test -p asset-hub-polkadot-runtime governance_and_nontransfer_proxy_allow_multi_asset_bounties
cargo test -p asset-hub-kusama-runtime governance_and_nontransfer_proxy_allow_multi_asset_bounties
```

pr_details.title: Add support for kick_member in pallet-society and key deposit and proof of ownership in staking-async-rc-client
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body:
This PR
- bumps pallet-society to introduce `kick_member` as per https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11280
- adds support for proof as input parameters (but no real validation since https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/1739 has not been backported to `stable2512`  ) as per https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11255
-  adds support for storage keys deposit on staking-async-rc-client as per https://github.com/paritytech/polkadot-sdk/pull/11242 (10 DOT for Polkadot AssetHub, 1 KSM for Kusama AssetHub)

## TODO 

- [ ] (if there is time) re-run benchmark for pallet-society on asset-hub-kusama. Not critical since kick_member is meant to be used once in a lifetime but nice to have
- [x]  CHANGELOG

Instead, if you have already modified it, simply delete the following line. -->

pr_details.author: ggwpez
pr_details.body:

- Add sanity origin check for the `EPMB::manage` call

[x] Does not require a CHANGELOG entry

pr_details.title: Allow fellowship's Architects track (ranks 4+) to manage fellowship treasury and salary on Asset Hub
pr_details.author: franciscoaguirre
pr_details.body:
Changed the XCM configuration from Asset Hub and Collectives to allow:
- Architects track to use the scheduler
- Architects track to dispatch calls as treasurer = `[Plurality { body: BodyId::Technical, part: BodyPart::Voice }, GeneralIndex(4)]`
  - This location semantically means "The 4th rank inside of the Technical Fellowship. I expect we can use this same pattern for other ranks.
- Architects origin to alias (with `AliasOrigin`) to the fellowship's treasury and salary pallets on Asset Hub

This allows better internal management of the fellowship treasury, without needing to go to OpenGov's Root track.

pr_details.title: Asset Hubs: add reserves info to support non-teleportable Foreign Assets
pr_details.author: acatangiu

pr_details.author: sigurpol

pr_details.title: Change Secretary Salary Location
pr_details.author: Doordashcon
pr_details.body: Removes the separate Secretary salary paymaster and directs all Secretary salary payments through the existing Fellowship salary account.

pr_details.title: Fix upgrade guide for parachains and provide a script for running proposals
pr_details.author: bkchr
pr_details.body:
This fixes the runtime upgrade guide. It was using an incorrect block number to schedule calls on parachains. On parachains we are using the relay parent block numbers and not the chain local block numbers. Leading to proposals never getting executed in the dry run.

To simplify the entire process, a script is provided that does all the steps automatically.

Instead, if you have already modified it, simply delete the following line. -->

pr_details.title: Restrict StakingOperator proxy to explicit utility batch calls
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body: Replace the `RuntimeCall::Utility { .. }` wildcard with explicit `batch`, `batch_all`, and `force_batch` calls only. The wildcard unnecessarily exposed `as_derivative`, `dispatch_as`, and `with_weight` which have no legitimate use for staking operations, and future utility pallet additions would be automatically exposed.

pr_details.title: Change PET branch used in CI tests
pr_details.author: rockbmb
pr_details.body:
https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1065 introduced changes to runtimes that PET needs to handle for tests to not fail spuriously; this PR changes its branch to point to the `test.yml` repository to avoid spurious failures.

The branch is based on [this PR](https://github.com/open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests/pull/524) by @sigurpol that updates PET to handle the changes to `pallet-staking/palletproxy`.

pr_details.title: ci: add check-runtime validation to release workflow
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body:
Runs [@polkadot-api/check-runtime](https://github.com/polkadot-api/check-runtime) against each chain's WASM produced by the build step to detect metadata, runtime API, and metadata-hash issues.

It runs between build-runtimes and ecosystem-tests, for each PR's `check-runtimes` step. 

Fix #1083. 

Example (run with a test workflow within this PR) [here](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/actions/runs/22327638424/job/64609453574?pr=1086).

The workflow could  be further simplified once/if [check-runtime tool](https://github.com/polkadot-api/check-runtime) returned a specific error if a node is unreachable (see related issue in the check-runtime repo [here](https://github.com/polkadot-api/check-runtime/issues/2))

- [x]  Does not require a CHANGELOG entry

pr_details.title: Preparation for Release 2.1.0
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body:
🚨🚨🚨 **THIS IS NOT THE FINAL  RELEASE PR** 🚨🚨🚨

This branch aims to prepare `Release 2.1.0` for  Polkadot (see  [release tracker](https://github.com/orgs/polkadot-fellows/projects/1/views/3)),  which is expected to land on chain no later than March 14th. We will release on Kusama 1 week before.

Notables changes that will be merged **after** this PR is merged:


Main content of this PR:
- bump Polkadot SDK from 2507 to 2512
  -  It builds on top of @pandres95 's awesome work in #951  - thanks Pablo and everyone who has contributed there :bow:

Some of key changes:
-   Upgrade SDK to `stable2512` (currently `stable2512-2-rc1`
-   Introduction of the first version of DAP pallet
-  All staking changes needed for [DAP phase 1](https://forum.polkadot.network/t/the-roadmap-for-the-dynamic-allocation-pool-dap/16511): session keys on AH, nominator fast-unbondanble and not slashable via config, no more treasury burns, slash burns redirected to DAP.  All these changes are Polkadot-only

## Notes

In retrospect, this PR should have solely focused on bumping the SDK to 2512. The scope became excessive, and I apologize for this.

## TODO:

In the scope of this PR:
- [x] review breaking changes coming with the bump to  sdk `2512`
- [x]  re-run benchmarks also for `pallet_staking_async` and  `pallet_staking_async_rc_client` (brand new on KAH and PAH!)

**After this PR is merged and before creating 2.1.0 release PR**,  we also still want the following PRs to land:

- @acatangiu 's [PR](https://github.com/sigurpol/runtimes/pull/5):  Full integration of SDK #9948 in fellowship runtimes for 2.1.0 release
- @dhirajs0's [PR](https://github.com/sigurpol/runtimes/pull/4) to support multi assets bounties

pr_details.title: Refactor PET in `test.yml` CI workflow
pr_details.author: rockbmb
pr_details.body:
Closes #1010 .

This PR will rework the PET job in `.github/workflows/test.yml`.

- [x] use a test matrix with individual retry/timeout configurations rather than just `yarn test` to avoid restarting the whole suite on a single suite's failure
- [x] To also avoid RPC timeouts due to throttling/stalling by test network's production RPC endpoints, consider using https://github.com/AcalaNetwork/subway or Chopsticks (as in https://github.com/open-web3-stack/polkadot-ecosystem-tests/pull/504)
- [x] Filter end-to-end test suites from PET to be used in finalized workflow
    -  This repository is only concerned with the runtimes that are under the Polkadot Fellowship's purview, while PET contains end-to-end tests for other ecosystme chains; as such, it makes sense to filter what is included in CI to further reduce load.
- [x] Re-enable parts of `test.yml` that were disabled to test PET changes in isolation

# Review notes

Main changes:
* new folder in `.github/subway-configs`; it contains [Subway](https://github.com/AcalaNetwork/subway) configs for the RPC proxies that will be created during CI: one for each Polkadot/Kusama relay + system parachain .
* PET reenabled in `.github/workflows/test.yml`, and completely refactored
    - it uses a matrix strategy with two jobs, one for each network: Polkadot and Kusama

## Details

Each of the above P/K jobs:
- Fetches Rust toolchain + Subway, builds its binary, and starts a Subway proxy for each of that network's relay/SP
       - Polkadot: 1 proxy for relay + 5 proxies for SPs
       - Kusama: 1 proxy for relay + 4 proxies for SPs
- Downloads WASM artifacts from a previous step of the CI pipeline
- Overrides PET's `.env` with said runtimes + proxies' endpoints
    - That means PET will run using runtimes built against the code of whichever PR triggered CI, and cache RPC responses, which are shared between all chains' tests
- Gets `yarn`, checks out PET repo, builds it
- Runs PET's known block number command from within it, to use the latest state in combination with built WASM runtimes
- Runs all end-to-end test suites for that network's chains
    - only runs tests related to relay + SPs; ecosystem chains are skipped, as this repo's purview is only for Fellowship-maintained runtimes
- Cleans up Subway proxies at the end

In particular, the critical change:

```bash
          yarn test -u $TEST_FILES --pool=threads --maxWorkers=3 --retry=3
          # `$TEST_FILES` contains all E2E modules for the network: relay + SPs included
          # 1. `-u` updates snapshots so that they don't fail spuriously e.g. proof size/ref time changes
          # 2. `--pool=threads` runs test files from `$TEST_FILES` in parallel using worker threads. Default
          #    is `--pool=forks`, which uses child processes - heavier.
          # 3. `--maxWorkers=3` combined with the above means at most 3 test files running concurrently, each in its
          #    own thread
          # 4. `--retry=3` attempts a failing test 3 times. This operates at the level of individual tests, not modules,
          #    or the whole test suite; one failure will *not* require a full rerun.
```

---

- [x] Does not require a CHANGELOG entry

pr_details.title: [Encointer] a fix and new pallets offline-payment and reputation-rings
pr_details.author: brenzi
pr_details.body:
This PR introduces new features and a fix for Encointer:

medium-urgent fix:
* fix community treasury stablecoin swap option enactment, (replacing https://github.com/sigurpol/runtimes/pull/7)

new features:
* enable payments while offline, settle later when online. [docs](https://book.encointer.org/protocol-offline-payments.html), [code](https://github.com/encointer/pallets/tree/master/offline-payment)
   * Our community in Tanzania has recently suffered a internet shutdown for several days. This prepares us for the next temporary outage.
   * Based on ZK e-cash Groth16 proofs (arkworks)
* enable anonymous proof-of-personhood. [docs](https://book.encointer.org/protocol-reputation-rings.html), [code](https://github.com/encointer/pallets/tree/master/reputation-rings)
   * Based on ring-VRF (similar but different from people registry design).


* [x] await benchmark bot
